_meta.note: 19th major-arc enrichment dip; first of the A-roundup campaign (Matt S154). Stannis is heavily pre-wired (409 internal edges) but causally ISLANDED (only 10 internal causal edges) — the Class-D profile. WIRE+ENRICH. 3 new nodes (burning-of-the-seven-at-dragonstone, shadow-killing-of-cortnay-penrose, leeching-of-edric-storm — NO container tag, Dragonstone/Renly's-war, matches the A2 arcs). 31 edges. Marquee value = the causal/MOTIVATES substrate the spine lacks: the R'hllor conversion engine (lit lightbringer's 0-edge island + the missing stannis WORSHIPS rhllor + melisandre ADVISES stannis), the second shadow (Cortnay), the Renly-kinslaying guilt MOTIVATES, the why-march-north (Davos's argument), the RR/twincest seam (murder-of-jon-arryn MOTIVATES stannis). Node bodies live in the mint script. THEORY-GATED (node-prose, NO edges): Azor Ahai / king's-blood magic / stone dragon / Shireen-sacrifice. No leeching FORESHADOWS/CAUSES the 3 kings' deaths.

edges.quote: The red woman walked round the fire three times
edges.note: Melisandre conducts the burning of the Seven (praying in three tongues).

edges.quote: grasped the sword with his gloved hand, and wrenched it free of the burning wood
edges.note: Stannis draws the red sword from the pyre — the central participatory act of his public conversion.

edges.quote: Queen Selyse echoed the words
edges.note: Selyse joins the R'hllor responses (already WORSHIPS rhllor in graph; her role in THIS event is new).

edges.quote: He felt ill as he watched them burn, and not only from the smoke
edges.note: Davos watches the burning; the load-bearing perception that seeds his opposition to Melisandre.

edges.quote: jade-green flames swirling around cherry-red steel
edges.note: Lightbringer drawn from the fire — the climax of the ceremony. Fixes lightbringer's 0-edges-graph-wide.

edges.quote: Stannis drew his longsword. Lightbringer, Melisandre had named it
edges.note: Stannis bears Lightbringer (the glowing-but-heatless 'red sword of heroes'). The false-Lightbringer reading stays node-prose.

edges.quote: It is time I tried another hawk
edges.note: Stannis's public conversion to R'hllor (the 'red hawk' metaphor); pragmatic/instrumental, private doubt -> Tier-2. No stannis WORSHIPS rhllor existed (only selyse).

edges.quote: Now I will hear hers
edges.note: Stannis dismisses Cressen's counsel to hear Melisandre's -- the advisory role. NO melisandre ADVISES edge existed (the single most-named missing edge).

edges.quote: she was naked, and huge with child
edges.note: Melisandre births the second shadow in the sea cave -- the agent. A DISTINCT event from shadow-assassination-of-renly (Renly died in his tent earlier).

edges.quote: Ser Cortnay will be dead within the day
edges.note: Stannis orders it (knows the outcome, sends Davos to row Mel in) -- orderer who did not personally execute.

edges.quote: steering a tiny boat with a black sail
edges.note: Davos rows Melisandre into the sea cave -- the logistical participant (not combatant).

edges.quote: Ser Cortnay will be dead within the day
edges.note: Cortnay Penrose is the target (found dead the next day; the castle yields).

edges.quote: He knew that shadow
edges.note: Davos sees the shadow slip into the castle and recognizes Stannis in it -- the perception that turns him against Mel.

edges.quote: The seaward side of Storm’s End perched upon a pale white cliff
edges.note: The sea cave beneath Storm's End.

edges.quote: command of Storm’s End would pass to this stripling
edges.note: Cortnay's death passes command to the green Lord Meadows, who yields -> Stannis takes Storm's End. De-islands taking-of-storms-end (had only 1 PART_OF). ENABLES (Meadows' free choice to yield).

edges.quote: Then my answer is still no, my lord
edges.note: Cortnay refuses to surrender Edric Storm to Stannis -- and dies for it. The protective stance that is the whole reason the shadow is sent.

edges.quote: only the king’s shadow shifting against the silken walls
edges.note: Catelyn is in the tent and SEES the shadow strike Renly -- the basis for her naming Stannis the killer.

edges.quote: cried Brienne the Blue when she saw that evil flow
edges.note: Brienne is holding Renly as he dies and SEES the shadow -- grounds her vengeance arc.

edges.quote: It was Brienne,” insisted Lord Caron
edges.note: The FALSE in-world accusation (Caron, on Emmon Cuy's oath) -- the whodunit misdirection. Brienne is innocent; Stannis (AGENT_IN) is the true killer. Parallels the RR Jon-Arryn false-blame pattern.

edges.quote: I believe it was Lady Stark who slew the king
edges.note: The other FALSE in-world accusation (Florent). The misdirection layer on the shadow-killing -- Catelyn is innocent.

edges.quote: I dream of it sometimes. Of Renly’s dying
edges.note: MARQUEE: the Renly-kinslaying GUILT thread. The islanded renly-s-death-reflection node (cIn=0/cOut=0) now drives Stannis's haunted severity. MOTIVATES -> character.

edges.quote: a king protects his people, or he is no king at all
edges.note: MARQUEE why-march-north: Davos's 'save the realm' argument pivots Stannis toward the Wall. The MOTIVATES the spine lacked (it had only retreat ENABLES move-to-wall).

edges.quote: I will have justice for him. Aye, and for Ned Stark and Jon Arryn as well
edges.note: CROSS-CONTAINER SEAM (RR/wo5k -> Stannis): Arryn (whom Stannis investigated the twincest WITH) was murdered; Stannis vows justice, the root posture of his claim. De-islands murder-of-jon-arryn (only outbound was lysa-accuses-tyrion).

edges.quote: the red woman retrieved the silver dish and brought it to the king
edges.note: Melisandre conducts the king's-blood leeching ritual.

edges.quote: The leech was twisting in the king’s grip
edges.note: Stannis names and throws each leech ('the usurper Joffrey/Balon/Robb') -- a co-agent in the naming ritual.

edges.quote: The boy’s blood, Davos knew. A king’s blood
edges.note: The leeches are fat with Edric's king's-blood -- he is the victim.

edges.quote: Davos watched her lift the lid
edges.note: Davos witnesses the leeching -- the horror that drives the rescue.

edges.quote: within the great round room called the Chamber of the Painted Table
edges.note: The Chamber of the Painted Table at Dragonstone, where the leeching is performed.

edges.quote: The boy’s blood, Davos knew. A king’s blood
edges.note: The horror of the leeching drives Davos to smuggle Edric off Dragonstone -- the leeching node's causal-OUT. MOTIVATES -> character.

edges.quote: He is aboard a Lyseni galley, safely out to sea
edges.note: Davos smuggles Edric Storm off Dragonstone (to Lys) against the sacrifice -- the counter-move to the leeching.

edges.quote: Eat their bread and salt, drink their ale
edges.note: Jon counsels Stannis how to win the northern mountain clans (the bread-and-salt advice) -- the de-facto northern-advisor role; no jon ADVISES stannis edge existed.
